title: strychnos asterantha
intro: Strychnos asterantha, also known as the African strychnine tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It contains a toxic alkaloid called strychnine, which has been traditionally used in medicine for its purgative and anthelmintic properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as intestinal worms and constipation. The health benefits of Strychnos asterantha include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve flatulence, and act as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often dried and processed to create medicinal powders or decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
